metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace or augment AC2 with a self-contained proving command (e.g. a fixture representing the known-good state) so it is verifiable in-session; if e991-e347-3444-427c is a hard prerequisite, declare it as a blocking dependency edge in the plan.
findings.finding: AC2 states the check 'passes on current main once bug e991-e347-3444-427c lands' but provides no proving command, no description of what 'current main after that bug' looks like, and no dependency edge to that ticket — making this criterion unverifiable in-session without the executor tracking an external ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: State the default: 'AC2 is gated on e991-e347-3444-427c; add a fixture file (infra/compose/test-fixtures/docker-compose-good.yml + compose-up-good.sh) that mirrors the post-fix state and assert `bash check-volume-drift.sh test-fixtures/docker-compose-good.yml test-fixtures/compose-up-good.sh` exits 0.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a sequencing note in the plan body (e.g. in Scope or a Dependencies section) stating that the check must be merged after or together with bug fix e991-e347-3444-427c, and reframe AC2 to verify the deliverable's behavior on a known-good fixture rather than relying on an external fix landing.
findings.finding: AC2 references an external prerequisite bug fix ('e991-e347-3444-427c') that is not described anywhere in the plan body, making AC2 an orphan criterion that verifies a prerequisite state rather than the deliverable itself — and leaving no instructions for how the deliverable and the prerequisite should be sequenced.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Dependencies' section: 'This check must be merged after bug e991-e347-3444-427c; until that fix lands, the check will report a false failure on main.' Rewrite AC2 as: 'Check passes when run against a fixture docker-compose.yml and compose-up.sh that are in sync (no drift).' This decouples the criterion from an external fix.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit sequencing constraint (e.g. 'this ticket must merge after e991-e347-3444-427c') or restate AC2 as a testable condition on a known-clean fixture rather than a dependency on an in-flight fix.
findings.finding: AC2 ('Check passes on current main once bug e991-e347-3444-427c lands') is conditioned on an external, not-yet-landed fix, so the 'passing' end-state has no observable proof until that separate change merges. Between this change landing and e991-e347-3444-427c landing, main will be broken by this new gate, yet the plan provides no mitigation or sequencing constraint for that window.
findings.suggested_fix: Restate AC2 as: 'Check passes when run against the compose files as they will exist post-e991-e347-3444-427c (verify on that branch or add a clean fixture); enforce merge ordering so this gate lands no earlier than e991-e347-3444-427c.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify (or prescribe) that the bug-fix for e991-e347-3444-427c structures compose-up.sh's volume creation section so that full volume names appear as literal, greppable strings — and document the expected grep pattern in this plan.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the new check can verify each external volume name 'appears in compose-up.sh's creation list', but compose-up.sh currently builds volume names via a `for d in git index cache db etc logs plugins reviewbot; do ... gerrit_${d}` pattern loop — full volume names like `gerrit_reviewbot_tickets` never appear as literal strings. The bug-fix ticket (e991-e347-3444-427c, status: open) changes this to a 'volume:dir pair mapping', but the plan does not specify what that structure looks like or how grep will match literal volume names against it post-fix. The grep-match strategy against compose-up.sh's creation list is an unverified assumption.
findings.location: What section: 'appears in compose-up.sh's creation list'; infra/scripts/compose-up.sh lines 61-78

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact grep/awk pattern used to extract external volume names from docker-compose.yml's multi-line YAML structure (e.g. `awk '/^[^ ]/{vol=$1} /external: true/{print vol}' docker-compose.yml`), verifying it against the actual file format.
findings.finding: The plan claims a 'grep-level script' suffices to extract external volume names from docker-compose.yml, but the actual file uses a multi-line YAML block where the volume name and `external: true` flag are on separate lines (e.g. `gerrit_git:` then `  external: true`). A simple `grep 'external: true'` yields no volume names; extracting volume names requires a multi-line pattern (e.g. `grep -B1` or `awk`). The plan does not specify what grep pattern handles this, leaving the core parsing logic unverified and potentially incorrect.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope section: 'Extract volume names with `awk` or `grep -B1`: e.g. `awk '/^  [a-z]/{name=$1} /external: true/{print name}' infra/compose/docker-compose.yml` (strips trailing colon). This is still dependency-free and handles the actual two-line YAML block format.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify (in the plan or the script's own header) the exact grep/extraction patterns for both files, and add a self-test or canary assertion that the check detects a known-bad fixture so pattern rot is caught immediately.
findings.finding: The grep patterns that match external volume names in docker-compose.yml and creation calls in compose-up.sh are unspecified. A grep-level heuristic that silently drifts out of sync with either file's format (e.g., indentation change, inline comments, variable substitution, multi-line volume blocks) will produce false negatives — exactly the failure mode the check is meant to prevent — with no indication the gate has become ineffective.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Grep-level script' / AC: 'Check fails when an external volume is added'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a mandatory self-test step to the script: pipe a known-bad synthetic snippet through the check and assert it fails, so any pattern regression surfaces in CI rather than silently producing false negatives.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the regression-test mechanism for AC1: either name the fixture files (e.g. `infra/compose/test-fixtures/docker-compose-drifted.yml`) that simulate the 10837ca88 diff shape, or state the exact command (`bash check-volume-drift.sh <fixture>` exits non-zero) so the regression test is deterministically runnable.
findings.finding: AC1 references a 'regression test on 10837ca88's diff shape' but provides no concrete proving command or fixture description — an executor cannot determine whether this means running the check against a specific commit, a synthetic fixture file, or a git-diff simulation.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC1: 'Proven by: `bash infra/compose/check-volume-drift.sh infra/compose/test-fixtures/docker-compose-with-undeclared-volume.yml infra/compose/compose-up.sh` exits non-zero (exit code 1).' Provide the fixture file as part of the PR.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Justify or remove the 'no YAML parser dependency' constraint: python3+yaml is already available in config-check.sh, and the grep-level approach cannot reliably parse synthesised volume names from compose-up.sh's loop structure (`gerrit_${d}`) — document the specific grep patterns that will be used and verify they correctly handle both loop-created and explicit `docker volume create` calls (including the post-e991-e347-3444-427c shape of compose-up.sh).
findings.finding: The plan mandates a 'grep-level script' with 'no YAML parser dependency,' but python3 with PyYAML is already a confirmed dependency used in the same config-check.sh (check 3: `python3 -c "import yaml,sys; yaml.safe_load(...)"`). The no-parser constraint is unjustified and actively harmful: the current compose-up.sh uses a `for d in git index cache db etc logs plugins reviewbot; do ... gerrit_${d}` pattern where volume names are synthesised at runtime — a grep over this loop body cannot reliably reconstruct the full creation list. The gerrit_reviewbot_tickets volume (the exact bug) can't be expressed as `gerrit_${d}` with `d` from that list, which is why bug e991-e347-3444-427c exists. A Python-based approach parsing both files properly would be both more reliable and consistent with the existing toolchain.
findings.location: ## Scope — 'Grep-level script, wired into the existing config-check seam; no YAML parser dependency.'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the constraint with: 'Use python3 (already a config-check.sh dependency) to parse external volume names from docker-compose.yml via yaml.safe_load, then grep/awk compose-up.sh for each literal volume name as a string. This is robust to both the loop pattern and explicit create calls, and requires no new dependency.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace or augment the 'no YAML parser dependency' rationale with one that is accurate given config-check.sh already uses python3+yaml; either justify grep on other grounds (e.g., 'simpler to audit, no structured parse needed given the consistent block-form YAML') or explicitly allow a small Python snippet consistent with the existing check.
findings.finding: The plan's stated positive rationale for the grep approach — 'no YAML parser dependency' — is directly contradicted by the existing codebase: config-check.sh step 3 already uses `python3 -c 'import yaml,sys; yaml.safe_load(...)'` to validate docker-compose.yml. The plan therefore offers no defensible reason to prefer grep over a small Python snippet that would more robustly extract volume names (handling multi-line YAML, inline `{external: true}`, etc.).
findings.location: Scope section: 'Grep-level script, wired into the existing config-check seam; no YAML parser dependency.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving command for AC3, e.g. `make config-check` (with a drifted fixture in place) exits non-zero, confirming the new check is wired into the existing target.
findings.finding: AC3 ('Runs in CI via existing make target') has no proving command — there is no described step that demonstrates the new check is actually invoked by `make config-check` (or whichever target) and not merely present as a standalone script.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief doc artifact (CLAUDE.md entry, ADR, or inline README note in infra/compose/) recording the external-volume pairing policy and linking to Incident 2731, so the rationale survives beyond this ticket.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new enforcement policy — every external volume in docker-compose.yml must have a compose-up.sh counterpart, enforced at CI — but no ADR, CLAUDE.md entry, or design-doc update is planned. Future contributors adding infrastructure volumes will not know this constraint exists until CI fails with no documented rationale.
findings.location: ## Why / ## What / Acceptance Criteria — no documentation step mentioned
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-paragraph entry to infra/compose/README.md (or CLAUDE.md) stating: 'Every external: true volume in docker-compose.yml must have a creation block in compose-up.sh; this is enforced by make config-check (see Incident 2731).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name `infra/scripts/config-check.sh` explicitly as the file to be edited (add a new numbered check section there), rather than only referencing `make config-check`.
findings.finding: The plan names `make config-check` as the integration seam but never names `infra/scripts/config-check.sh` as an edit target, even though that is the concrete file that must be modified to add the new check.
findings.location: Scope section: 'wired into the existing config-check seam'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, change 'Grep-level script, wired into the existing config-check seam' to 'Add check 5 to `infra/scripts/config-check.sh` (the script invoked by `make config-check`): grep external volume names from docker-compose.yml and assert each appears in compose-up.sh's creation list.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` as a consumer of `make config-check` and note that the new check must not land before bug e991-e347-3444-427c closes (to avoid breaking the Verified gate on all open changes).
findings.finding: `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` is an unenumerated consumer of `make config-check` that will also gate on the new check, but is not mentioned in the plan.
findings.location: AC: 'Runs in CI via existing make target'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC1: either name a concrete test mechanism (e.g., a bats test that injects a dummy external volume into a fixture compose file and asserts non-zero exit) or reword it as 'the check would have caught the drift introduced by commit 10837ca88' (a design-time assertion, not a runnable test).
findings.finding: AC1 references commit `10837ca88` as a 'regression test on 10837ca88's diff shape,' but no test file, fixture, or mechanism is named in the plan that exercises the check against that commit's diff. The plan creates only a grep script, with no stated test harness.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'Check fails when an external volume is added to docker-compose.yml without compose-up.sh support (regression test on 10837ca88's diff shape).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the grep check handles shell-variable-expansion patterns in compose-up.sh (e.g., `gerrit_${d}` loop); either prohibit the loop pattern in compose-up.sh going forward (and assert that in the check or a comment) or extend the check to also scan for loop variable lists that would expand to the expected volume name.
findings.finding: The grep-level mechanism produces a false negative whenever a future developer adds a new external volume to compose-up.sh via the existing `gerrit_${d}` loop pattern (e.g., adding `reviewbot_foo` to `for d in ...`), because the composite string `gerrit_reviewbot_foo` never appears literally in the file and a grep for it would fail to match, causing the check to falsely report drift as 'covered'.
findings.location: Scope section / Mechanism description ('Grep-level script'); also compose-up.sh lines 61–78
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the Scope or What section: 'compose-up.sh must use explicit `docker volume create gerrit_<name>` calls (not the `gerrit_${d}` loop) for all external volumes — the grep check relies on literal name presence. The check should also assert the loop covers only volumes that are no longer declared external: true if any remain loop-provisioned.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that volume-creation commands in compose-up.sh must be idempotent (e.g., skip-if-exists guard or `|| true` pattern), and add an AC that re-running compose-up.sh on a pre-provisioned environment is a no-op / non-fatal.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that compose-up.sh must contain a creation entry for every external volume, but never specifies that those creation commands must be idempotent (e.g., `docker volume create` fails if the volume already exists unless guarded). A re-run of compose-up.sh on an environment where volumes are already present could error out, meaning the provisioning seam the CI gate is designed to protect is itself not guaranteed safe to re-invoke.
findings.location: Scope / Acceptance Criteria — 'Check passes on current main once bug e991-e347-3444-427c lands'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'Re-running compose-up.sh when external volumes already exist must be a no-op (exit 0).' Implement with: `docker volume inspect <name> >/dev/null 2>&1 || docker volume create <name>` or equivalent in compose-up.sh, and add a test case for this in the regression suite.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
